skill.name: ckm:slides
skill.description: Create strategic HTML presentations with Chart.js, design tokens, responsive layouts, copywriting formulas, and contextual slide strategies.
skill.summary: Strategic HTML presentation design with Chart.js data visualization, responsive layouts, and proven copywriting formulas.
skill.author: nextlevelbuilder

security_audit.summary: Skill is a documentation/template-only skill for creating HTML presentations. Static analyzer flagged markdown code examples as suspicious, but evaluation confirms all findings are false positives. Backticks are used for code formatting in shell command examples, not actual execution. The Chart.js CDN URL is legitimate. No executable code that poses security risk was found.

low_findings.title: Documentation references search helper script
low_findings.description: Multiple markdown files reference a Python search helper script (search-slides.py) with example shell commands. These are documentation examples showing users how to search the slide knowledge base, not executable code in the skill itself. Files: copywriting-formulas.md, layout-patterns.md, slide-strategies.md.

low_findings.title: External CDN dependency in HTML template
low_findings.description: The HTML template loads Chart.js from cdn.jsdelivr.net. This is a well-known legitimate CDN, but it introduces a third-party dependency. Users should verify CDN integrity for production use.

content.value_statement: Creating professional slide decks with data visualization requires expertise in design, copywriting, and structure. This skill provides ready-to-use HTML templates, Chart.js integration, and proven formulas to build persuasive presentations quickly.
actual_capabilities: Generate complete HTML presentation decks with 16:9 aspect ratio and responsive breakpoints
Integrate Chart.js for line, bar, doughnut, and radar visualizations
Apply 25 copywriting formulas matched to slide types and emotion goals
Use 14 layout patterns with CSS grid structures and animation classes
Select from 15 proven deck structures including YC Seed, Series A, and Sales Pitch
Follow Nancy Duarte Sparkline pattern for engaging narrative flow
limitations: Requires user to provide topic and content; skill generates structure and templates
Chart.js is loaded from external CDN requiring internet access
No built-in export to PPTX or PDF format
Templates produce static HTML; no slide editor or live preview

use_cases.title: Startup pitch decks
use_cases.description: Founders can quickly create YC Seed or Series A decks with proper emotion arcs and investor-focused structure.

use_cases.title: Sales presentations
use_cases.description: Sales teams can build persuasive decks with problem-solution structure, social proof, and clear CTAs.

use_cases.title: Internal business updates
use_cases.description: Managers can produce QBRs, all-hands, and board meeting decks with consistent design and data visualization.

prompt_templates.title: Create a 10-slide pitch deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a pitch deck for [your product] using the YC Seed structure. Include problem, solution, traction, market size, product demo, business model, team, financials, and ask slides.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a sales pitch presentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a 9-slide sales pitch for [your product] targeting [customer persona]. Include personalized hook, their problem, cost of inaction, your solution, proof, differentiators, pricing, objection handling, and CTA.

prompt_templates.title: Design a data-heavy QBR
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a quarterly business review with 12 slides covering KPIs, metrics dashboard, progress against goals, blockers, and next quarter priorities. Include Chart.js visualizations for revenue and growth trends.

prompt_templates.title: Build a product demo deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 6-slide product demo presentation for [your product]. Include hook, problem, solution overview, live demo screenshots, key features, benefits, and call to action.

output_examples.input: Create a 10-slide pitch deck for a B2B SaaS analytics platform
output: Title slide with gradient headline and tagline
Problem slide using PAS formula about data blind spots
Traction slide with line chart showing MRR growth
Market size slide with bar chart comparison
Product demo slide with screenshot and key features
Business model slide with pricing tiers
Team grid with founder photos and backgrounds
The Ask slide with clear funding amount and use of funds

output_examples.input: Generate a sales pitch for an enterprise security tool targeting CISOs
output: Problem statement about breach costs and compliance burden
Cost of inaction slide quantifying downtime losses
Customer case studies with ROI metrics
Next steps with demo scheduling CTA

best_practices: Match copywriting formula to slide emotion goal - use PAS for problems, FAB for features, AIDA for CTAs
Follow Duarte Sparkline pattern alternating between current pain and future possibility
Use 16:9 aspect ratio container with letterboxing for consistent viewing on any screen
Limit each slide to one key message with supporting visual or metric
Add progress bar and keyboard navigation for self-paced viewing
anti_patterns: Avoid wall-of-text slides - break dense content into multiple focused slides
Do not use more than 2 charts per slide - overwhelms the viewer
Never skip the emotion arc - flat decks lose audience engagement
Avoid mixing incompatible design systems - stick to one color palette and font pairing

faq.question: What output format does this skill produce?
faq.answer: The skill generates a complete HTML file with embedded CSS and JavaScript that can be opened in any browser. It is not compatible with PowerPoint or Keynote.

faq.question: Does it require internet access?
faq.answer: Yes, the template loads Chart.js from a CDN. For offline use, download Chart.js locally and update the script tag.

faq.question: Can I customize the color scheme?
faq.answer: Yes, the template uses CSS variables for all colors. Replace the design tokens at the top of the style block to match your brand.

faq.question: How many slides can a deck have?
faq.answer: There is no hard limit. The included strategies recommend 6-15 slides for most use cases. Workshops can have up to 40 slides.

faq.question: Does it support animations?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes fade-up, scale, count, stagger, and pulse animation classes that can be applied to any element.

faq.question: Can I export to PDF?
faq.answer: Use your browser print function (Ctrl+P) and select Save as PDF. For best results, set margins to none and enable background graphics.
